  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `╬Ż` to diagonal
entries and `╬╝` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : ÔäĽ) : Index n Ôćĺ Measure ÔäŁ :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ╬Ż
    else ╬╝


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : ÔäĽ) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist ╬╝ ╬Ż n)

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n ╬╝ ╬Ż X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i Ôëá j) :
  HasLaw (fun ¤ë ÔćŽ X ¤ë i j) ╬╝ P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ┬Ě -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ┬Ě -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun ¤ë ÔćŽ X ¤ë j i) ╬╝ P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ÔłÇ ¤ë, X ¤ë i j = X ¤ë j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros ¤ë; exact Y.symm i j ¤ë
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext

    exact min_le
